criteria.name: feature_correctness
criteria.description: All core product requirements work as specified, including routine CRUD, step CRUD, timer execution, automatic step transitions, streak tracking, XP calculation, level progression, achievements, analytics, and visual progression.

criteria.name: timer_engine_accuracy
criteria.description: Timer remains accurate across long sessions, pause/resume cycles, backgrounding, browser tab inactivity, reloads, and step transitions. Auto-advance behavior is reliable and free of timing drift.

criteria.name: routine_step_crud_ux
criteria.description: Users can create, edit, delete, duplicate, reorder, and manage routines and steps directly from the dashboard with minimal friction. Mobile-friendly duration inputs and intuitive interactions are provided.

criteria.name: gamification_design
criteria.description: XP, levels, streaks, best streak tracking, achievements, and visual progression form a compelling reward loop that encourages repeat usage without overwhelming the user.

criteria.name: analytics_dashboard
criteria.description: Dashboard clearly presents current streak, best streak, XP progress, completed routines, total focus time, achievement progress, and weekly completion analytics with low cognitive load.

criteria.name: mobile_first_ux
criteria.description: UI follows mobile-first principles. Touch targets are at least 44x44px, controls are thumb-zone optimized, spacing prevents accidental taps, and core actions are reachable with one hand.

criteria.name: accessibility
criteria.description: WCAG AA contrast standards are met. Interface supports keyboard navigation, semantic HTML, screen readers, visible focus states, scalable text, and color-independent status indicators.

criteria.name: responsive_design
criteria.description: Layouts adapt correctly across phones, tablets, and desktop widths without overlap, clipping, horizontal scrolling, broken navigation, or unreadable content.

criteria.name: performance
criteria.description: Application loads quickly, maintains smooth animations, minimizes layout shift, avoids unnecessary rendering, and provides responsive interactions under normal usage.

criteria.name: state_management_persistence
criteria.description: Application state persists correctly using localStorage. XP, streaks, achievements, analytics, and visual progression are restored accurately after refreshes or browser restarts.

criteria.name: technical_reliability
criteria.description: Application handles unexpected failures gracefully, validates user input, prevents crashes, and provides meaningful recovery paths when storage or runtime errors occur.

criteria.name: edge_cases
criteria.description: Handles empty routines, zero-duration steps, invalid durations, duplicate names, deleted active routines, reset confirmation flows, interrupted sessions, corrupted localStorage, and first-run scenarios.

criteria.name: interaction_polish
criteria.description: Micro-interactions, progress animations, achievement unlocks, XP gains, transitions, loading states, and error messages feel polished, consistent, and purposeful.

criteria.name: visual_hierarchy
criteria.description: Information architecture is clear. Timer remains the primary visual anchor, progress indicators are distinguishable, and users can quickly understand current status and next actions.

criteria.name: code_quality_architecture
criteria.description: Code is modular, maintainable, reusable, documented where necessary, and follows clear separation of concerns without excessive duplication.
